publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-08-07 18:26:26 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Letters from colleagues, public figures, friends, relatives, and others concerning the execution and sale of various works, his nomination to membership in honorary and professional societies and related professional and social matters; an autobiographical sketch (typescript carbon), 1943, membership certificates and citations, numerous photographs of the artist and his sculpture, newspaper clippings on his career, and miscellaneous printed items. Also, messages of condolence and formal tributes sent to his widow, Cecelia W. Muench MacNeil, obituaries, and press reports concerning a memorial established for the artist. Correspondents include Adriaan Jacob Barnouw, Emile Brunet, Jo Davidson, William Henry Fox, Carl Paul Jennewein, Leon Kroll, and Victor Emanuel III.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters from colleagues, public figures, friends, relatives, and others concerning the execution and sale of various works, his nomination to membership in honorary and professional societies and related professional and social matters; an autobiographical sketch (typescript carbon), 1943, membership certificates and citations, numerous photographs of the artist and his sculpture, newspaper clippings on his career, and miscellaneous printed items. Also, messages of condolence and formal tributes sent to his widow, Cecelia W. Muench MacNeil, obituaries, and press reports concerning a memorial established for the artist. Correspondents include Adriaan Jacob Barnouw, Emile Brunet, Jo Davidson, William Henry Fox, Carl Paul Jennewein, Leon Kroll, and Victor Emanuel III.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Hermon Atkins MacNeil papers, #2425. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a printed finding aid.

did.unittitle: Bryan Lathrop, Holland House, Kinsley, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M. n.p. Lathrop has purchased two of MacNeil's bronzes -Primitive Chant: and Navaho Orator - and wishes to know how many copies are in existence or to be issued.

did.unittitle: Orson Lowell, College Point, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DS to M., n.p. Handwritten contract wherein Lowell agrees to pay $30 a month for a studio in New York City.

did.unittitle: Walter L. Palmer, 5 Lafayette St., Albany, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. To M., n.p. Congratulates him for having won the Albany Competition.

did.unittitle: Karl Bitter (b.1867 - d.1915), University of Michigan, Ann Arbor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations

did.unittitle: Lorado Taft, 1038 Fine Arts Building, Chicago, Ill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Albany competition congratulations; also, thanks him for sending the facts about an exhibition in New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations.

did.unittitle: Isidore Konti, 154 West 55th Street, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for sending photographs of the Albany monument; mentions that Professor Hamlin has sailed for England.

did.unittitle: Charles Mulford Robinson (b.1869 - d.1917) The National Arts Club, Gramercy Park.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Has had his shop redecorated. Miss McDougall, associate editor of The Designer, visited him. He had just returned from a trip to Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Comments on a bronze bust McNeil sold. Amusing water color sketch at top of sheet.

did.unittitle: W.H. Holmes, Smithsonian Institute, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TLS to M., 45 West 22nd Street, New York City. Asks if he would design and execute a gold medal provided for by Alexander Agassiz to be awarded to persons making important deep-sea investigations. 2pcs.

did.unittitle: William H. Taft, The White House, Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TLS to M., College Point, New York. Asks him to design a gold medal (doesn't say for what in the letter)

did.unittitle: Edwin Howland Blashfield, (b.1848-d.1925) 48 Central Park South, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to Mr. Breck, n.p. Comments on the difficulty of a problem he had sent him.

did.unittitle: George Henry Bissell (b.1829 - d.1920) Mount Vernon, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to Mr. and Mrs. M., n.p. Reminisces about the Latin Quarter in Paris and his days in the army and navy. Refers to himself as Peri Bissell.

did.unittitle: Paul Wayland Bartlett (b.1850 - d.1925) 239 Randolph Place, N. E., Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., College Point, N.Y. Thanks him for a postal card.

did.unittitle: Daniel Chester French (b.1850 - d.1931), 125 West Eleventh Street, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TLS to M., College Point. Informs him that the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City wants to buy a bronze copy of his Sun Vow. Asks if he wants the plaster cast back.

did.unittitle: Daniel Chester French (b. 1850 - d. 1931), 125 West Eleventh Street, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TLS to M., College Point. The plaster cast will be returned as soon as he wants it.

did.unittitle: Edwin Howland Blashfield, 48 Central Park South, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Suggests that the National Academy of Design try to find a new person to be president instead of himself.

did.unittitle: Edwin Howland Blashfield, 48 Central Park South, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Accepts nomination for the presidency.

did.unittitle: George Haven Putnam (b.1844 - d.1930), 24 Bedford Street, Strand, W.C. 2, London, England

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TLS to M., College Point. Discusses the possible nomination of Bertelli to the Century Association

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DS to M., n.p. To Hermon A. MacNeil fellow member of the American Academy of Arts and Letters.

did.unittitle: Robert Underwood Johnson (b.1853 - d.1937, c/o Mr. F.H. Holden, New Milford, Connecticut, R.F.D. 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Discusses the nomination of Stephen Vincent Benet to the Academy of Arts and Letters. Feels some people might vote against him. Walter Johannes Damrosch (b.1862 - d.1950) is also being considered.

did.unittitle: Robert Underwood Johnson (b.1853 - d. 1937), Hall of fame, New York University , New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Discusses further the election of people to the Academy - votes required, etc.

did.unittitle: Anna Huntington, La Pyrole, Leysin, Switzerland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a letter congratulating her on her election to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. Comments on the difficulties of being an artist. (written on the back of a photograph of Alpine and snow scene).

did.unittitle: Edwin Howland Blashfield, 50 Central Park West, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Thank-you note for a visit to MacNeil's home.

did.unittitle: MacKenzie King, Office of the Prime Minister, Ottawa, Canada

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TLS to M., College Point, New York. Thanks him for a letter. Hopes he will visit Canada again soon. + envelope

did.unittitle: James Truslow Adams, Sheffield House, Southport, Connecticut.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: TLS to M., College Point, N.Y. Discusses a museum in South Dakota.

did.unittitle: George Grey Barnard (b.1863 - d.1938), 700 Fort Washington Avenue, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a letter and invites him and his wife to his home.

did.unittitle: John Burroughs (b.1837 - d. 1921), West Park, N.Y.,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a photo of a bust MacNeil had executed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A petition signed by MacNeil, other sculptors, and various associates who subscribed money for a fund to send Miss Alice Simpson to Europe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from a scrapbook, compiled by Hermon A. MacNeil's secretary. Scrapbooks dismantled, material placed in bond folders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possible sister or sister-in-law of Mrs. MacNeil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 letter to Hermon, 2nd enclosed to Cel

scopecontent.p: Director and Manager of Grand Central Art Galleries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosed letter from Mr. MacNeil to Emile Brunet dated 1945-10-05

scopecontent.p: Dept. of Germanic Languages, Columbia University (Adrian Jacob Barnouw, b. 1877

scopecontent.p: Written before Mr. MacNeil's death, during his illness

scopecontent.p: Letter to Miss Mutochle

scopecontent.p: Artist and student of Mr. MacNeil's

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Secretary, Art Commission Associates, letter and enclosed resolution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President, Conrad Poppenhusen Association, letter and enclosed resolution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assistant to the President, American Academy of Arts and Letters. Invitation to a meeting where a Tribute to Mr. MacNeil will be read.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning marriage of Cecilia W. Muench to Hermon A. MacNeil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To Mom and Dad

did.unittitle: I.d. Correspondence in scrapbook compiled by MacNeil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Executive Secretary, The American Institute of Architects; notification of MacNeil's election as honorary member of the Institute

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Secretary, The American Numismatic Society. Letter informing him of his unanimous election to the society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Secretary, National Sculpture Society, letter informing Mr. MacNeil of his election as an Honorary Fellow

scopecontent.p: Probably Helen half-sister, of Mr. MacNeil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Built by John Clinton MacNeil, Hermon MacNeil on porch, father and oldest sister Anie to left of him and brother Caleb in distance. dull, mounted, Everett, Massachusetts

scopecontent.p: Photo of striding peasant woman with child and ax sculpture. Large, dull photo, unmounted.

did.unittitle: III.a. Clippings dealing with The American Academy in Rome

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: College at Rome requests funds; lists Hermon A. MacNeil as outstanding alumni

did.unittitle: Sculptor H.A. MacNeil Win Professorship at Rome Dec. 27
Hermon A. MacNeil to be Professor of American Academy at Rome

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes MacNeil's work on exhibit at the American Academy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Honored for professorship in Rome.

did.unittitle: III.b. Clippings concerning the Liberty 25-Cent Piece
How Eagles on American Coins Fly. And How! But Do You Really Care?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses the question of what an eagle does with its feet when it flies. Cites H.A. MacNeil, designer of quarter. Boston is single credit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cites MacNeil in question of how eagles fly; New York (AP) single credit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses MacNeil's Liberty quarter

scopecontent.p: Josephine Litchfield and Henry Turner Baily

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carol and Hermon MacNeil, standing in front of their home

did.unittitle: Post Card - George R. Clark Memorial

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From HBG, Vincennes, Indian to H.A.MacNeil

did.unittitle: Envelope for Memorial Post Card - Pony Express

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably Bridal. Discarded 12-68

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discarded 12-68

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graham Taylor

did.unittitle: 2 Metropolitan Museum of Art Membership cards for Mrs. MacNeil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Henry Turner Bailey, reprinted from the School Arts Book

did.unittitle: Letter to MacNeil, College Point, Long Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From William Henry Fox, Secretary General, U.S. Commission to the International Exposition of Art and History at Rome, stating that the King of Italy wishes to buy his bronze statuette, A Primitive Chant

did.unittitle: Letter to MacNeil from R.W. Woolley (Xerox copy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woolley, Director of the Mint, Washington, D.C., notifying MacNeil his designs submitted for the Quarter Dollar had been approved.

did.unittitle: Letter to MacNeil, from Willard W. Beatty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beatty, Superintendent, Bronxville Public Schools, Bronxville, N.Y. accepting MacNeils offer of an indefinite loan of the plaster cast of the sculpture Sun Vow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dull finish photo of MacNeil seated on a stone bench at his summer home, Bronze statue to his right and relief behind him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Glossy photo of MacNeil with clay model of his Ezra Cornell Statue, (1915 or earlier)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of bust of Judge Douglass Boardman of Ithaca.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 x 5 card giving locations the sculpture Sun Vow had been exhibited (1928 & undated))
